2606.22178.title: DSSCNet: A Transfer Learning Framework for Cross-Corpus Dysarthric Speech Severity Classification
2606.22178.paper_summary: Dysarthric speech severity classification is challenging due to speaker variability, class imbalance, and limited datasets. This study introduces DSSCNet, a deep learning model that employs transfer learning and multi-corpus learning to enhance speaker-independent classification. By pre-training on one dysarthric speech corpus and fine-tuning on another, DSSCNet achieves improved feature extraction and cross-corpus generalization. Experimental results demonstrate that DSSCNet outperforms state-of-the-art models for speaker-independent severity classification, achieving 75.80\% accuracy on TORGO and 68.25\% on UA-Speech, significantly reducing misclassification errors. The findings confirm that leveraging knowledge transfer between datasets improves model robustness, making DSSCNet well-suited for automated dysarthria assessment. This research contributes to the development of more effective assistive speech technologies for individuals with speech impairments.
2606.22178.paper_summary_zh: 語音障礙的語音嚴重程度分類因講者變異性、類別不平衡和有限的數據集而具有挑戰性。這項研究介紹了DSSCNet，一種深度學習模型，利用轉移學習和多語料庫學習來增強與講者無關的分類。通過在一個語音障礙語音語料庫上進行預訓練，並在另一個語料庫上進行微調，DSSCNet實現了改進的特徵提取和跨語料庫的泛化。實驗結果顯示，DSSCNet在與講者無關的嚴重程度分類中超越了最先進的模型，在TORGO上達到75.80\%的準確率，在UA-Speech上達到68.25\%，顯著減少了錯誤分類的次數。研究結果確認了在數據集之間利用知識轉移可以提高模型的穩健性，使DSSCNet非常適合自動化的語音障礙評估。這項研究有助於開發更有效的輔助語音技術，幫助有語音障礙的人士。
2606.22178.author: Arnab Kumar Roy et.al.
2606.22178.authors: Arnab Kumar Roy, Hemant Kumar Kathania, Paban Sapkota, Sudarsana Reddy Kadiri, Shrikanth Narayanan
